title: Shinji a Misogynist
wikitext:
[]: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Director Black » Mon Jan 02, 2017 3:00 pm Seeing a thread on the Madonna-Whore complex being applied to Asuka and Rei, it made me remember a theory that's been brought up in a few Eva analysis pieces (One on Foldable Ideas) I've seen and/or read: the interpretation that Shinji is sexist. One good analyst on Tumblr (Yes, they are surprisingly possible) explained of Shinji's demeaning attitude towards Misato and Asuka; the former has him commenting on how it's clear why someone like her is single at her age in the beginning of Episode 7 and the latter with scenes pointing to the beginning and end of EoE. Foldable Ideas felt that Shinji's need for Asuka was typical the Nice Guy persona. "Oh, if these women won't take care of me, then they must be the problem." I'd love to hear anyone's thought's on this...the former can be questioned since the examples she gives up are valid (She's redoing her blog), but the latter shows once again misunderstood Shinji is in the eyes of many Americans.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 02, 2017 3:05 pm No more so than the typical Japanese boy is, in all likelihood.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 02, 2017 3:30 pm Director Black wrote: the former has him commenting on how it's clear why someone like her is single at her age in the beginning of Episode 7

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Geometer » Mon Jan 02, 2017 3:43 pm I could see Shinji growing up to be a resentful "nice guy" type in the future if he had never had become a pilot.He might already bit of a gender traditionalist (what do you expect from a 14 year old really?) but I don't think Misato or Asuka being more lady-like would have made Shinji more comfortable around them. He still pins most of his hate on himself, not to others including women. Of course you can't just hate yourself and not others. So as he gets older his self hate would probably bleed more and more on others, including women for "not giving him a chance" or "ignoring the nice guy for bad boys", etc. He wouldn't go about it in a "get my a beer honey" way but would probably be quite snide and mean around women in passive aggressive ways.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Joseki » Mon Jan 02, 2017 3:45 pm That's an "analysis" that totally miss the point of those scenes and of the dialog in EoE where Shinji accuses of being the problem. He's fighting escapism, he always put the blame on others regardless of their gender, and there's a rather large amout of fights with Gendo to prove it. Regarding the statements about Misato I don't even know why that's supposed to be something a "misogynist" would say. She has a lot of problems, she's drinks a lot, lives in a pile of junk, eat unhealthy stuff all the time. She also has big problems with men thanks to her father. In real world that's a good amount of reason to not find a partner. I also suspect it was mainly a joke. Honestly I don't even know what to say about this "theory". It twist the meaning of some random scenes and dialogue, ignore contest just for being Tumblr-y.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Director Black » Mon Jan 02, 2017 6:15 pm Joseki wrote: Honestly I don't even know what to say about this "theory". It twist the meaning of some random scenes and dialogue, ignore contest just for being Tumblr-y.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Ray » Mon Jan 02, 2017 6:24 pm He definitely has issues. . . but I don't think he HATES women. Maybe when as he got older and progressively more bitter (read, like his father) then maybe he'd have a interest in them ONLY to gratify himself (like Gendo with Ritsuko). But he wouldn't go out of his way to hurt them. I mean, think about it. The first woman in his life his mother, died on him. Then the other women in his life alternatively try to seduce him, put on airs around him, and treat him like dirt. Sometimes in the same span of time. Shinji is in many ways the most extreme version of a MGTOW. A guy who, having had such bad experiences with women, being used, tossed aside, or outright emasculated by the women in his life. Would just opt out of a relationships with women in general to avoid being hurt to the extremes he was in the past.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 02, 2017 6:38 pm As with the Madonna-Whore bits for the girls, I don't think Shinji's mature enough for this to even be a relevant question. He might certainly become a misogynist in the future, at least if he didn't go through Instrumentality, but as of the time in the show it seems like quite a stretch.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Director Black » Mon Jan 02, 2017 8:09 pm Bagheera wrote: He might certainly become a misogynist in the future, at least if he didn't go through Instrumentality, but as of the time in the show it seems like quite a stretch.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 02, 2017 10:43 pm It's basically been said already, but no. Shinji is not a misogynist; he's fourteen. He's got a lot to learn about people in general, not just women, although as a teenage boy, discovering the opposite sex is a significant part of his character. This kinda reminds of his conversation with Kaji, wherein the latter attempts to explain the difference between men and women, and Shinji basically responds with "I just don't understand adults."

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Kendrix » Tue Jan 03, 2017 11:24 am Tumblr, for all its good sides, does have a tendency to ovranalyze. Discrimination is rampant; Everybody knows much is terribly wrong & how desperately it needs fixing but no one's really sure how and sometimes that leads to reactionary witchhunts more than rational discussions of wether something is actually wrong & how to fix it. Also I it's necessary to differentiate between things that are wrong/mistakes, things that make you a jerk, and things that are outright abusive/reprehensible. Another tendency they have is to become overly fixted on little details at the expense of a pattern (One teasing comment about Misato's very real sloppyness doesnot mean he holds these sort of seriously heldopinions; It's like, a joke among quasi-family members, and it's not like he finds her unatractive or is all that surprised when she does get back together with Kaji. ) Rei, Asuka and Misato are all soldiers and the relatively tradion-inclined Hikari is right there. So while it's important to have this discussion, i do NOT think that he can be called a misogynist nor "doomed to become one" or anything like that. He's frustrated that he drew a bad lot in life & has a complex about being rejected/abandoned that extends into all his relationships, but he doesn't really - quite the opposite he tries hard not to have expectations at all in a futile attempt to spare himself the experience of dissapoinment. Shinji's young, inexperienced and does not really have a solid core of beliefs and principles because he's governed by a wound borne out of rejection . For the most part in his everyday life, Shinji respects Misato & Rei & aknowledges their strenght and doesn't ever really treat them or anyone else as lesser or less human because they're girls or doubt their competence etc. Consider that he works in a plae full of female authority figures (Misato, Ritsuko, Hikari at school) and doesn't really clash or resent them for that. The worst I could think off is that ultra cringey moment in ep 16 where he's like "Fighting is for men" but he does this mostly as a response to Asuka's teasing of how he oughta act "like a man" and as such is part of him trying to please/placate people by saying what they want and the afore mentioned lack of a solid core more that something he actually believes or reflects in his everyday life (It's telling that Misato & Ritsuko react like, "Ugh teenagers, seems like I'll have to have a talk with him afterwards.") - Indeed he doesn't mind doing all the cooking & housework and doesn't expect the girls to do it, nor did their less than ladylike behavior keep him from becoming close with Misato & Rei. Asuka is a different case because the two have been antagonistic to each other from day one. She sees him mostly as a rival and he just wants to get along with everyone; The teenage lust further complicates that/ makes him take her rejections harder What mild sense of comradery there may have been during the late action arc evaporates the moment he trumps her synch rate; His attempts to get along are feeble and half-hearted whereas she outright bullies him or at least hammers him with non-constructive criticism at the mildest provocations. There's a a lot of bad blood between the two, one way or another. They have opposite personalities & were forced into close quarters by their duties; There would still be if Asuka were a dude. How he treats her is not in any way indicative of what he things of women in general. Which is not to say that there weren't a few dub con incidents where he did thngs you just shouldn't do even with your rivals; But the first was minor (the ep 9 thing) and the second happened when his sanity meter had pretty much hit the bottom, just after he'd been forced to off kaworu and attempted to drown himself. It seems to miss the point; Normally as a kid you'd have the crucial experience of being told by your parents "I still love you but what you did was wrong" and in response to that you learn not to do wrong things, but for that you need the foundation that there's no existential threat, that you won't be abandones - Shinji never had that, he had this seminal experience of both his parents just dissapearing in short sucession without leavinh him an explanation and got stuck in a mindset of 'How do I not anger that person so they don't reject me' and certainly some resentment that it has to be this way. So though he's generally fairl benevolent & has a good heart he doesn't really have hard lines he won't cross even if he's having a particular bad day. But he's also fairly young and only just entering the stage where you'd form your own beliefs in the first place. I guess it's just another example of how this "Nice Guy/friend zone" phenomenon really ruins everything for everyone, I just wished the pick up artists that coined it had never existed - There really are those horrible MRM idiots who are terrifying in their self-serving rationalizations, entitlements and the way they fail to get how faking a friendship in order to get sex is emotinal manipulation - But now it gets slapped onto ANY kind of 'unrecruited crush' plot where the male doesn't turn off his feelings with a button, regardless of wether he gets entitled/petty/jealous or not. He might totally respect her & value their friendship, but he still gets slapped with it. Like, can't I just have my tragic emo courtly love plots in peace? *sigh*. Besides it dillutes the much needed criticism of ~real~ entitlement. As it happens with a lot of labels that once described actual things/phenomena but then got indiscrimately slapped onto anything one doesn't like ("Mary Sue", "Pixie Dream Girl" etc.)

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 03, 2017 2:34 pm Honestly Asuka seems to be the one doing the most to promote such ideas in some sort of perverse self-hatred loop, which is exactly why Shinji's teasing in that episode hits home. Despite all her efforts at maintaining the facade deep down Asuka just wants 'a real man' to swoop in and take control of her life for her, and is continually frustrated that Shinji doesn't fit the bill (nice guy labeling from her) and Kaji doesn't see her that way.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jan 03, 2017 3:16 pm Shinji's not a misogynist, he's a teenager. He's still learning about the world while his mind is being warped by puberty and bombarded by Eva responsibilities. Very few teens, male or female, don't think in a way that isn't selfishly painting the opposite sex with broad strokes. Asuka says some pretty misandristic things to Shinji as well, calling him a "house-broken male" to his face during the times when he respects Misato's desicions. It's just teenage angst that, hopefully, would have been cleared up when the two finally would have reached adulthood.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Bagheera » Tue Jan 03, 2017 3:19 pm Yeah, Asuka has some extremely gendered attitudes. It's one of her greatest flaws as a character. She would ideally have mellowed out as she matured, but, well . . .

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jan 03, 2017 5:08 pm Well a lot of what I might say has already been covered by Kendrix but: One of the things is that people refer to sexism, misogyny, male chauvinism, patriarchy and so on like they’re all interchangeable. Time to critique this discussion up! These all come in many different subtly distinct forms. It’s the same with masculinity. According to ‘hegemonic masculinity’ theory, there are many things that are for boys and exclude women traditionally - like being sceintist - but physical displays of strength, organised militarism, etc. are the hegemonic ‘true’ masculinity that all the others refer to. A lot of people would recognise that in Shinji. He has a very male-coded persona of a shy, put-upon, slightly stuffy and secretly pervy geek that recognisably a very ‘male’ archytpe even though it’s also not truly ‘manly’. I also think the Nice Guy critique has been thinned by cliche status. It’s obvious how some people can use it as crutch to disavow hegemonic aggressive masculinity while smuggling in equally patriarchal sense of sexual entitlement. It's not unusual for Foldable Ideas to incorporate the common ‘EoE-is-anti-otaku’ and objecting to porny exploitation culture as part of a feminist spin on a similar idea. Weirdly, I kinda agree that Shinji's anger expresses an explosion of his unhappy relationship with women but I don't think the show wants us to think of it as a feminist problem with a feminist response. I take it the tumbler the OP is citing is qmisato? Likewise, I agree with her that Shinji does have a guilty, repressed mode of interacting with women that expresses itself in public timidness and secret resentment. I just think it’s starts as an emotional problem rather than a personal expression of misogyny/objectification, etc. I actually think feelings like Shinji’s are common in real life and part of the oppression of women, but only because social structures ensure that men’s personal neuroses can impose themselves on women. The reason I think this matters is because Kwame Ture nee Stokely Carmichael said “If a white man doesn’t like me that’s his problem. If a white man can lynch me that’s my problem. Racism is not a question of attitude it’s a question of power” Simply, if Shinji or whole generations of men have personal problems it wouldn’t mean anything if domination of women didn’t allow them to apply it to women. This is one of the big problems of fiction-based political criticism is that it’s allowed to ignore that subtle entity called real life entirely, never mind real power relations. Individually they’re still just personal problems. It’s only through socialist analysis that one can transform into the other without really ‘secretly being in fact actually’ the other. If I wanted to say Shinji was or was not a misogynist these are the qualifiers I would use. Structurally: 1. Does Shinji have an unhealthy relationship with women? I think so but I’m trying to prove it on the other thread. 2. Does Shinji reproduce conventional gender roles? Well, in the manga before he gets a flashback of Yui’s contact experiment he insists that she’s a ‘normal mom’. Given the other flashbacks usually show show her being a housewife that’s probably what he means. So yes! Unlike the episode 16 thing I think he actually means it! 3. Does Shinji consciously resent women? Almost certainly not. 4. Is Shinji part of the organised and conscious repression of women? Like he has time for that. One thing to home in on: why would Shinji really resent women at all? I think he idolizes Yui, doesn't really get Misato, pities/is intrigued by Rei, and yeah he butts heads with Asuka. Maybe that time Asuka makes him do all the work when they're busting back into NERV during the Matarael attack. But that's it.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Director Black » Tue Jan 03, 2017 9:51 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: I take it the tumbler the OP is citing is qmisato? Likewise, I agree with her that Shinji does have a guilty, repressed mode of interacting with women that expresses itself in public timidness and secret resentment.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Squigsquasher » Thu Jan 05, 2017 3:57 pm ...No? Why the fuck would he be? Micro-Rant  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Thu Jan 05, 2017 7:16 pm I tend to agree with all of what Squigsquasher said in his micro-rant. Shinji just isn't one. How what he said to Misato is anything other than paying her back for her teasing she does to him by pointing out no less or more than the truth I don't think I could ever understand. The truth hurts sometimes, and Misato can't really refute what he said, she pushed Kaji away cause she felt se was falling in love with her father. I honestly don't see Shinji as being capable of hating anyone other than himself or his dickbag of a father. Even when Asuka ignores him he still tries, Misato he sees as a big woman-child, and Rei he has some sort of adoration/pity for. Anything else I could mention has been mentioned already by a few people. He cooks, he cleans, he's essentially the housewife and Asuka is the abusive angry husband who shouts for him to get back in the kitchen and do his job, which would also make Misato the useless child who can't get their life together. So he can't be a mysoginst if he's taken over and assumed the opposite gender role. I only ever heard him complain that Misato doesn't do her share, and that's also like a wife complaint that her husband is leaving her with all the work. Moving on to a different point - in EoE, before instrumentality, he looks for Asuka's support and doesn't get it (then that scene happens which is just vile and wrong), and even in instrumentality, when he hates the whole damn world and tangs everyone out of spite and malice, he finds Asuka's soul and stil trys to gain her acceptance/approval. So I don't think someone looking for the adoration of women could be a mysoginst on its very definition. But if you're really looking for a misogynist in NGE, Kensuke would be your best bet, the reason being, those pictures the perv takes in Episode 9 of the girls changing are from a fucking tree, clearly he has no respect for women. Gendo isnt a misogynist either, he's causing instrumentality to get the love of his life back, and he's just an asshole and treats everyone like shit, save for Yui and Rei. Finally, Tumblr throws that term around more than a baseball during a triple play. For every cat post on tumblr, there is probably one about men being bastards. I saw it in my feed for years before I stopped logging in.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Jan 06, 2017 6:21 am Agreed on basically all counts, except Kensuke. I mean sure, the picture-taking was pretty skeevy but at the end of the day, he IS still 14. Everyone does shitty things when they're 14, and one slightly tasteless activity during adolescence shouldn't damn one forever (especially not when the whole sequence took place during the silliest part of the Action Arc and was almost certainly played exclusively for laughs). Pervert? Probably (but again, horny teenager). Woman hater/horrible person? No.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Fri Jan 06, 2017 7:14 am Squigsquasher wrote: Agreed on basically all counts, except Kensuke. I mean sure, the picture-taking was pretty skeevy but at the end of the day, he IS still 14. Everyone does shitty things when they're 14, and one slightly tasteless activity during adolescence shouldn't damn one forever (especially not when the whole sequence took place during the silliest part of the Action Arc and was almost certainly played exclusively for laughs). Pervert? Probably (but again, horny teenager). Woman hater/horrible person? No.

Foldable Ideas felt that Shinji's need for Asuka was typical the Nice Guy persona. "Oh, if these women won't take care of me, then they must be the problem." I'd love to hear anyone's thought's on this...the former can be questioned since the examples she gives up are valid (She's redoing her blog), but the latter shows once again misunderstood Shinji is in the eyes of many Americans.

So though he's generally fairl benevolent & has a good heart he doesn't really have hard lines he won't cross even if he's having a particular bad day. But he's also fairly young and only just entering the stage where you'd form your own beliefs in the first place. I guess it's just another example of how this "Nice Guy/friend zone" phenomenon really ruins everything for everyone, I just wished the pick up artists that coined it had never existed - There really are those horrible MRM idiots who are terrifying in their self-serving rationalizations, entitlements and the way they fail to get how faking a friendship in order to get sex is emotinal manipulation - But now it gets slapped onto ANY kind of 'unrecruited crush' plot where the male doesn't turn off his feelings with a button, regardless of wether he gets entitled/petty/jealous or not. He might totally respect her & value their friendship, but he still gets slapped with it. Like, can't I just have my tragic emo courtly love plots in peace? *sigh*. Besides it dillutes the much needed criticism of ~real~ entitlement. As it happens with a lot of labels that once described actual things/phenomena but then got indiscrimately slapped onto anything one doesn't like ("Mary Sue", "Pixie Dream Girl" etc.)

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jan 03, 2017 5:08 pm Well a lot of what I might say has already been covered by Kendrix but: One of the things is that people refer to sexism, misogyny, male chauvinism, patriarchy and so on like they’re all interchangeable. Time to critique this discussion up! These all come in many different subtly distinct forms. It’s the same with masculinity. According to ‘hegemonic masculinity’ theory, there are many things that are for boys and exclude women traditionally - like being sceintist - but physical displays of strength, organised militarism, etc. are the hegemonic ‘true’ masculinity that all the others refer to. A lot of people would recognise that in Shinji. He has a very male-coded persona of a shy, put-upon, slightly stuffy and secretly pervy geek that recognisably a very ‘male’ archytpe even though it’s also not truly ‘manly’. I also think the Nice Guy critique has been thinned by cliche status. It’s obvious how some people can use it as crutch to disavow hegemonic aggressive masculinity while smuggling in equally patriarchal sense of sexual entitlement. It's not unusual for Foldable Ideas to incorporate the common ‘E

Article: Shinji a Misogynist
 by cliche status. It’s obvious how some people can use it as crutch to disavow hegemonic aggressive masculinity while smuggling in equally patriarchal sense of sexual entitlement. It's not unusual for Foldable Ideas to incorporate the common ‘EoE-is-anti-otaku’ and objecting to porny exploitation culture as part of a feminist spin on a similar idea. Weirdly, I kinda agree that Shinji's anger expresses an explosion of his unhappy relationship with women but I don't think the show wants us to think of it as a feminist problem with a feminist response. I take it the tumbler the OP is citing is qmisato? Likewise, I agree with her that Shinji does have a guilty, repressed mode of interacting with women that expresses itself in public timidness and secret resentment. I just think it’s starts as an emotional problem rather than a personal expression of misogyny/objectification, etc. I actually think feelings like Shinji’s are common in real life and part of the oppression of women, but only because social structures ensure that men’s personal neuroses can impose themselves on women. The reason I think this matters is because Kwame Ture nee Stokely Carmichael said “If a white man doesn’t like me that’s his problem. If a white man can lynch me that’s my problem. Racism is not a question of attitude it’s a question of power” Simply, if Shinji or whole generations of men have personal problems it wouldn’t mean anything if domination of women didn’t allow them to apply it to women. This is one of the big problems of fiction-based political criticism is that it’s allowed to ignore that subtle entity called real life entirely, never mind real power relations. Individually they’re still just personal problems. It’s only through socialist analysis that one can transform into the other without really ‘secretly being in fact actually’ the other. If I wanted to say Shinji was or was not a misogynist these are the qualifiers I would use. Structurally: 1. Does Shinji have an unhealthy relationship with women? I think so but I’m trying to prove it on the other thread. 2. Does Shinji reproduce conventional gender roles? Well, in the manga before he gets a flashback of Yui’s contact experiment he insists that she’s a ‘normal mom’. Given the other flashbacks usually show show her being a housewife that’s probably what he means. So yes! Unlike the episode 16 thing

Article: Shinji a Misogynist
 before he gets a flashback of Yui’s contact experiment he insists that she’s a ‘normal mom’. Given the other flashbacks usually show show her being a housewife that’s probably what he means. So yes! Unlike the episode 16 thing I think he actually means it! 3. Does Shinji consciously resent women? Almost certainly not. 4. Is Shinji part of the organised and conscious repression of women? Like he has time for that. One thing to home in on: why would Shinji really resent women at all? I think he idolizes Yui, doesn't really get Misato, pities/is intrigued by Rei, and yeah he butts heads with Asuka. Maybe that time Asuka makes him do all the work when they're busting back into NERV during the Matarael attack. But that's it.

[]: Re: Shinji a Misogynist? Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Thu Jan 05, 2017 7:16 pm I tend to agree with all of what Squigsquasher said in his micro-rant. Shinji just isn't one. The truth hurts sometimes, and Misato can't really refute what he said, she pushed Kaji away cause she felt se was falling in love with her father. I honestly don't see Shinji as being capable of hating anyone other than himself or his dickbag of a father. Article: Shinji a Misogynist
 his dickbag of a father. Even when Asuka ignores him he still tries, Misato he sees as a big woman-child, and Rei he has some sort of adoration/pity for. Anything else I could mention has been mentioned already by a few people. He cooks, he cleans, he's essentially the housewife and Asuka is the abusive angry husband who shouts for him to get back in the kitchen and do his job, which would also make Misato the useless child who can't get their life together. So he can't be a mysoginst if he's taken over and assumed the opposite gender role. I only ever heard him complain that Misato doesn't do her share, and that's also like a wife complaint that her husband is leaving her with all the work. Moving on to a different point - in EoE, before instrumentality, he looks for Asuka's support and doesn't get it (then that scene happens which is just vile and wrong), and even in instrumentality, when he hates the whole damn world and tangs everyone out of spite and malice, he finds Asuka's soul and stil trys to gain her acceptance/approval. So I don't think someone looking for the adoration of women could be a mysoginst on its very definition. Gendo isnt a misogynist either, he's causing instrumentality to get the love of his life back, and he's just an asshole and treats everyone like shit, save for Yui and Rei. Finally, Tumblr throws that term around more than a baseball during a triple play. For every cat post on tumblr, there is probably one about men being bastards.
